questions.question: Which tectonic plates collided to form the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas were formed by the collision of the Indian Plate with the Eurasian Plate.

questions.question: Which Himalayan range contains the highest peaks and permanent snow?
questions.explanation: Himadri or the Great Himalayas contain the highest peaks.

questions.question: Which valley lies between the Himachal and Shiwalik ranges?
questions.explanation: Duns are longitudinal valleys between Himachal and Shiwalik ranges.

questions.question: Which river forms the world's largest delta?
questions.explanation: The Ganga-Brahmaputra river system forms the Sundarbans delta.

questions.question: Which physiographic division was part of the Gondwana land?
questions.explanation: The Peninsular Plateau was part of the ancient Gondwana landmass.

questions.question: Which plateau is known as the storehouse of minerals in India?
questions.explanation: Chotanagpur Plateau contains rich deposits of coal, iron and minerals.

questions.question: Which mountain range acts as the watershed between the Narmada and Tapi rivers?
questions.explanation: Satpura Range lies between the Narmada and Tapi rivers.

questions.question: Which is the highest peak of the Eastern Ghats?
questions.explanation: Mahendragiri is the highest peak of the Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through a rift valley?
questions.explanation: Narmada flows through a rift valley between the Vindhya and Satpura ranges.

questions.question: Which hills separate the Deccan Plateau from the coastal plains?
questions.explanation: Western Ghats separate the Deccan Plateau from the western coastal plains.

questions.question: Which is the only large river in the Indian Desert?
questions.explanation: Luni is the only large river flowing through the Indian Desert.

questions.question: Which type of sand dunes are most common in the Thar Desert?
questions.explanation: Barchans are crescent shaped dunes common in deserts.

questions.question: Which coastal region lies between Mangalore and Kanyakumari?
questions.explanation: Malabar Coast lies between Mangalore and Kanyakumari.

questions.question: Which rivers form the major deltas on the eastern coast?
questions.explanation: These rivers form large deltas on the eastern coast.

questions.question: Which strait separates India and Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: Palk Strait separates India from Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Which island group is located closer to Myanmar?
questions.explanation: Andaman and Nicobar islands lie close to Myanmar.

questions.question: Which island contains India's only active volcano?
questions.explanation: Barren Island in the Andaman Sea has an active volcano.

questions.question: Which region of India is known for heavy rainfall due to orographic effect?
questions.explanation: Western Ghats cause heavy rainfall on the windward side.

questions.question: Which plateau lies between the Narmada and the Tapi rivers?
questions.explanation: Satpura Plateau lies between the Narmada and Tapi rivers.

questions.question: Which hill range forms the eastern boundary of the Peninsular Plateau?
questions.explanation: Eastern Ghats form the eastern boundary of the plateau.

questions.question: Which physiographic division of India is most densely populated?
questions.explanation: Northern Plains support dense population due to fertile land.

questions.question: Which plateau lies to the east of the Chotanagpur Plateau?
questions.explanation: The Meghalaya Plateau lies east of the Chotanagpur Plateau.

questions.question: Which river island is located in the Brahmaputra River?
questions.explanation: Majuli island lies in the Brahmaputra River.

questions.question: Which physiographic division of India is known for extensive mineral resources?
questions.explanation: The Peninsular Plateau is rich in mineral deposits.

questions.question: Which physiographic division acts as a natural climatic barrier for India?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas block cold winds and influence India's climate.
